title: Garlicky Portuguese Onion Fried Rice
description: A savory and aromatic Portuguese dish made with crispy onion rings, garlic, and a hint of spice. Perfect for a quick and easy lunch or dinner.

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: use if egg-friendly is not required

instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they start to soften, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the saffron threads and smoked paprika, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked rice, kosher salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the rice is heated through and starting to brown.
Add the low-sodium chicken broth and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Transfer the fried rice to a serving platter and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
